summary: DHH comments on a price war between Danish retailers Bilka and El-Giganten that has driven the PlayStation 2 price down 50% in just 10 days. He calls the retailers' managers 'moronic' for engaging in a race to the bottom that any business student would recognize as destructive. While critical of the business logic, he acknowledges it's great for consumers. He notes he'd buy one himself if he didn't already have an idle Dreamcast.
key_points: Danish retailers Bilka and El-Giganten slashed PlayStation 2 prices from $300 to $160 in 10 days
DHH views aggressive price wars as irrational business behavior that destroys value
Consumer benefit from corporate irrationality is acknowledged as a silver lining
Early glimpse of DHH's opinionated, no-nonsense commentary style on business decisions
money_quotes: Moronic managers at Danish retailers Bilka and El-Giganten have embarked on a spectactular race to see who can lose the most money in the shortest time available.
While this will have even first-year business students shaking their heads in astonishment, it's great for consumers.
key_insight: Aggressive retail price wars may benefit consumers in the short term but represent fundamentally irrational business behavior.
